emotional_core: Confidence comes from solid foundation, not from rushing into advanced code.
visual_metaphor: Building blocks being carefully laid from concrete upward—clean tools, organized workspace, structured thinking preceding construction.
key_quotes: Your environment is the difference between 'I learned Python' and 'I can use Python to think clearly.'
Everything in the remaining nine tutorials builds on this structure. You don't have much syntax memorized yet. You have something better: a foundation.
Most of your learning will happen through errors. The people who give up are the ones who see an error and think 'I'm not smart enough for this.' The people who succeed treat errors as feedback.

story_frames.visual: Overhead shot of cluttered desk: multiple Python windows open, scattered code, frustrated developer at laptop
Close-up of hands typing terminal command 'python3 -m venv env' into clean shell
Split screen comparison: messy codebase folder structure on left, organized isolated environment on right with clear hierarchy
Diagram animation: large problem box breaking into four smaller pieces with labels (decompose, pattern, abstract, algorithm)
Code editor showing simple but real Python script: data structures, calculation loop, results saved to JSON file
Error message on screen in red, with annotation pointing to error type and explanation of what went wrong
Developer leaning back satisfied, clean organized workspace, checklist visible with all boxes checked off
